obligations.description: Catalog metadata update during migration (R30-R35) depends on catalog-replication (WD-06, not yet specified). Requirements R30-R35 must be revisited when catalog-replication is specified.

F02.R33 VIOLATED: readKeyIndexV2 does not validate blockIndex in [0, blockCount) or intraBlockOffset >= 0 at key-index read time. Corrupt values surface as IndexOutOfBoundsException (from CompressionMap.entry) or AssertionError/AIOOBE (from EntryCodec.decode assert), not the descriptive IOException R33 requires. Related to R40 PARTIAL.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F02 v2: code fix added bounds validation in readKeyIndexV2 + replaced assert with runtime check in EntryCodec.decode)

obligations.description: F02.R39g VIOLATED: when dictionaryMaxBufferBytes is exceeded, TrieSSTableWriter abandons dictionary training and falls back to plain-codec compression (dictBufferAbandoned=true) instead of failing with IOException as the spec requires. Either the writer should throw IOException or R39g should be amended to codify the graceful-fallback behavior.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F02 v2: R39g amended to codify graceful-fallback as the correct behavior)

obligations.description: F15.R27 VIOLATED: JsonParser uses mutual recursion (parseValue → parseObject/parseArray → parseValue) for nested structure traversal, despite the spec requiring "must not use recursion for depth traversal." The code comment at JsonParser.java:153-156 explicitly acknowledges the deviation, accepting bounded recursion (capped by R52 at maxDepth=4096) in exchange for simpler code. Resolution path: either rewrite as an explicit stack-based state machine in Materializer, or amend R27 to permit bounded mutual recursion with an explicit maxDepth cap.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F15 v3: Materializer rewritten to use explicit Deque<Frame> stack; parseObject/parseArray/currentDepth removed)
